volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 3.2.481 - Section 3.3.570)

sections.label: Section 3.2.481
sections.original: Adhuc Archylis quae assolent quaeque oportet
sections.translation: As yet , Archylis , all the customary symptoms which ought to exist toward recovery , I perceive in her . Now , in the first place , take care and let her bathe ; [*] then , after that , what I ordered to be given her to drink , and as much as I prescribed ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.485
sections.original: Date : mox ego huc revertar .
sections.translation: do you administer : presently I will return hither . ( To herself aloud .) By all that ’ s holy , a fine boy has been born to Pamphilus . I pray the Gods that he may survive , since the father himself is of a good disposition , and since he has hesitated to do an injustice to this most excellent young woman .

sections.label: Section 3.2.489
sections.original: Vel hoc quis non credat qui te norit abs te esse ortum ?
sections.translation: Even this , who is there that knows you that would not believe that it originated in you ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.489b
sections.original: Quidnam id est ?
sections.translation: Why , what is this ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.490
sections.original: Non imperabat coram quid opus facto esset puerperae :
sections.translation: She didn ’ t order in their presence what was requisite to be done for the woman lying in ; but after she has come out , she bawls from the street to those who are in the house . O Davus , am I thus trifled with by you ? Or pray , do I seem to you so very well suited to be thus openly imposed upon by your tricks ? At all events it should have been with pre-caution ; that at least I might have seemed to be feared , if I should detect it .

sections.label: Section 3.2.495
sections.original: Certe hercle nunc hic se ipsus fallit haud ego .
sections.translation: Assuredly , upon my faith , it ’ s he that ’ s , now deceiving himself , not I .

sections.label: Section 3.2.495b
sections.original: Edixin tibi ,
sections.translation: I gave you warning , I forbade you with threats to do it . Have you been awed ? What has it availed ? Am I to believe you now in this , that this woman has had a child by Pamphilus ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.498
sections.original: Teneo quid erret : et quid agam habeo .
sections.translation: I understand where he ’ s mistaken ; and I see what I must do .

sections.label: Section 3.2.498b
sections.original: Quid taces ?
sections.translation: Why are you silent ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.499
sections.original: Quid credas ? quasi non tibi renunciata sint haec sic fore .
sections.translation: What would you believe ? As though word had not been brought you that thus it would happen .

sections.label: Section 3.2.500
sections.original: Mihin quisquam ?
sections.translation: Any word brought to me ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.500b
sections.original: Eho an tute intellexti hoc assimulari ?
sections.translation: Come now , did you of your own accord perceive that this was counterfeited ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.500c
sections.original: Irrideor .
sections.translation: I am being trifled with .

sections.label: Section 3.2.501
sections.original: Renunciatum est : nam qui istaec tibi incidit suspicio ?
sections.translation: Word has been brought you ; for otherwise how could this suspicion have occurred to you ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.502
sections.original: Qui ? quia te noram .
sections.translation: How ? Because I knew you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.502b
sections.original: Quasi tu dicas factum id consilio meo .
sections.translation: As though you meant to say that this has been done by my contrivance .

sections.label: Section 3.2.503
sections.original: Certe enim scio .
sections.translation: Why , I ’ m sure of it , to a certainty .

sections.label: Section 3.2.503b
sections.original: Non satis me pernosti etiam qualis sim Simo .
sections.translation: Not yet even do you know me sufficiently , Simo , what sort of person I am .

sections.label: Section 3.2.504
sections.original: Ego non te ?
sections.translation: I , not know you !

sections.label: Section 3.2.504b
sections.original: Sed si quid narrare occoepi , continuo dari
sections.translation: But if I begin to tell you any thing , at once

sections.label: Section 3.2.505
sections.original: Tibi verba censes falso : itaque hercle nihil iam muttire audeo .
sections.translation: you think that deceit is being practiced upon you in guile ; therefore , upon my faith , I don ’ t dare now even to whisper .

sections.label: Section 3.2.506
sections.original: Hoc ego scio unum neminem peperisse hic .
sections.translation: This one thing I am sure of , that no person has been delivered here .

sections.label: Section 3.2.506b
sections.original: Intellexti .
sections.translation: You have discovered that ? Still , not a bit the less will they presently be laying the child [*] here before the door . Of this , then , I now warn you , master , that it will happen , that you may be aware of it . Don ’ t you hereafter be saying that this was done through the advice or artifices of Davus .

sections.label: Section 3.2.510
sections.original: Prorsus a me opinionem hanc tuam esse ego amotam volo .
sections.translation: I wish this suspicion of yours to be entirely removed from myself .

sections.label: Section 3.2.511
sections.original: Unde id scis ?
sections.translation: How do you know that ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.511b
sections.original: Audivi et credo : multa concurrunt simul
sections.translation: I ’ ve heard so , and I believe it : many things combine for me to form this conjecture . In the first place then , she declared that she was pregnant by Pamphilus ; that has been proved to be false . [*] Now , when she sees that preparations are being made for the wedding at our house , the maid-servant is directly sent

sections.label: Section 3.2.515
sections.original: Obstetricem arcessitum ad eam , et puerum ut afferret simul .
sections.translation: to fetch the midwife to her , and to bring a child at the same time . [*] Unless it is managed for you to see the child , the marriage will not be at all impeded .

sections.label: Section 3.2.517
sections.original: Quid ais ? Quom intellexeras
sections.translation: What do you say to this ? When you perceived that they were adopting this plan , why didn ’ t you tell Pamphilus immediately ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.519
sections.original: Quis igitur eum ab illa abstraxit nisi ego ? nam omnes nos quidem
sections.translation: Why , who has induced him to leave her , but myself ? For , indeed , we all

sections.label: Section 3.2.520
sections.original: Scimus quam misere hanc amarit : nunc sibi uxorem expetit .
sections.translation: know how desperately he loved her . Now he wishes for a wife . In fine , do you intrust me with that affair ; proceed however , as before , to celebrate these nuptials , just as you are doing , and I trust that the Gods will prosper this matter .

sections.label: Section 3.2.523
sections.original: Immo abi intro : ibi me opperire , et quod parato opus est para .
sections.translation: Very well ; be off in-doors ; wait for me there , and get ready what ’ s necessary to be prepared . ( DAVUS goes into the house .) He hasn ’ t prevailed upon me even now altogether to believe these things ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.525
sections.original: Atque haud scio an quae dixit sint vera omnia :
sections.translation: and I don ’ t know whether what he has said is all true ; but I deem it of little moment ; this is of far greater importance to me — that my son himself has promised me . Now I ’ ll go and find Chremes ; I ’ ll ask him for a wife for my son ; if I obtain my request , at what other time rather than to-day should I prefer these nuptials taking place ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.530
sections.original: Nam gnatus quod pollicitus est haud dubium est mihi
sections.translation: For as my son has promised , I have no doubt but that if he should prove unwilling , I can fairly compel him . And look ! here ’ s Chremes himself , just at the very time .

sections.label: Section 3.3.533
sections.original: Iubeo Chremetem .
sections.translation: I greet you , Chremes .

sections.label: Section 3.3.533b
sections.original: O , te ipsum quaerebam .
sections.translation: O , you are the very person I was looking for .

sections.label: Section 3.3.533c
sections.original: Et ego te .
sections.translation: And I for you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.533d
sections.original: Optato advenis .
sections.translation: You meet me at a welcome moment . Some persons have been to me , to say that they had heard from you , that my daughter

sections.label: Section 3.3.535
sections.original: Meam nubere tuo gnato : id viso tune an illi insaniant .
sections.translation: was to be married to your son to-day ; I ’ ve come to see whether they are out of their senses or you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.536
sections.original: Ausculta paucis : et quid ego te velim et tu quod quaeris scies .
sections.translation: Listen ; in a few words you shall learn both what I want of you , and what you seek to know .

sections.label: Section 3.3.537
sections.original: Ausculto : loquere quid velis .
sections.translation: I am listening ; say what you wish .

sections.label: Section 3.3.538
sections.original: Per te deos oro et nostram amicitiam Chreme
sections.translation: By the Gods , I do entreat you , Chremes , and by our friendship , which , commencing with our infancy , has grown up with our years ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.540
sections.original: Perque unicam gnatam tuam et gnatum meum ,
sections.translation: and by your only daughter and by my own son ( of preserving whom the entire power lies with you ), that you will assist me in this matter ; and that , just as this marriage was about to be celebrated , it may be celebrated .

sections.label: Section 3.3.543b
sections.original: Ah ne me obsecra :
sections.translation: O , don ’ t importune me ; as though you needed to obtain this of me by entreaty .

sections.label: Section 3.3.545
sections.original: Alium esse censes nunc me atque olim cum dabam ?
sections.translation: Do you suppose I am different now from what I was formerly , when I promised her ? If it is for the advantage of them both that it should take place , order her to be sent for . But if from this course there would result more harm than advantage for each , this I do beg of you , that you will consult for their common good , as though she were your own daughter , and I the father of Pamphilus .

sections.label: Section 3.3.550
sections.original: Immo ita volo itaque postulo ut fiat Chreme :
sections.translation: Nay , so I intend , and so I wish it to be , Chremes ; and I would not ask it of you , did not the occasion itself require it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.551b
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What is the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.552
sections.original: Irae sunt inter Glycerium et gnatum .
sections.translation: There is a quarrel between Glycerium and my son .

sections.label: Section 3.3.552b
sections.original: Audio .
sections.translation: I hear you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.553
sections.original: Ita magnae ut sperem posse avelli .
sections.translation: So munch so , that I ’ m in hopes they may be separated .

sections.label: Section 3.3.553b
sections.original: Fabulae .
sections.translation: Nonsense !

sections.label: Section 3.3.554
sections.original: Profecto sic est .
sections.translation: It really is so .

sections.label: Section 3.3.554b
sections.original: Sic hercle ut dicam tibi :
sections.translation: After this fashion , i ’ faith , I tell you ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.555
sections.original: Amantium irae amoris integratio est .
sections.translation: the quarrels of lovers are the renewal of love .

sections.label: Section 3.3.556
sections.original: Hem , id te oro ut ante eamus dum tempus datur ,
sections.translation: Well — this I beg of you , that we may prevent it . While an opportunity offers , and while his passion is cooled by affronts , before the wiles of these women and their tears , craftily feigned ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.560
sections.original: Uxorem demus : spero consuetudine et
sections.translation: bring back his love-sick mind to compassion , let us give him a wife . I trust , Chremes , that , when attached by intimacy and a respectable marriage , he will easily extricate himself from these evils .

sections.label: Section 3.3.563
sections.original: Tibi ita hoc videtur : at ego non posse arbitror
sections.translation: So it appears to you ; but I do not think [*] that either he can possibly hold to her with constancy , or that I can put up with it if he does not .

sections.label: Section 3.3.565
sections.original: Qui scis ergo istuc nisi periclum feceris ?
sections.translation: How then can you be sure of that , unless you make the experiment ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.566
sections.original: At istuc periclum in filia fieri grave est .
sections.translation: But for that experiment to be made upon a daughter is a serious thing —

sections.label: Section 3.3.567
sections.original: Nempe incommoditas denique huc omnis redit :
sections.translation: Why look , all the inconvenience in fine amounts to this — possibly , which may the Gods forfend , a separation may take place . But if he is reformed , see how many are the advantages :

sections.label: Section 3.3.570
sections.original: Principio amico filium restitueris ;
sections.translation: in the first place , you will have restored a son to your friend ; you will obtain a sure son-in-law [*] for yourself , and a husband for your daughter .
